title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple continues her conversation with Mrs. Fyne regarding the woman who failed to marry Walter Fyne. Mrs. Fyne explains that after the failed engagement, the woman married a widower and moved to St. Catherine's Villa near the hospital, though she eventually left her husband for another man. Despite this, Walter remained friends with the woman, frequently visiting her and playing with the child from her marriage; that child has since grown up and is now a member of the Reed family. Mrs. Fyne also mentions Jackie Avesford, a former office clerk who pursued Helen Kennedy to improve his social status and who now operates the Avesford Water Lily Carriage Company. During this discussion, Walter Fyne enters the room, and Miss Marple observes him to be a refined, quiet, and somewhat boring man.

Meanwhile, Gwendolyn and Jules travel to Anstey Manor, a desolate white house situated on a barren hill, to investigate Colonel Richard Erskine’s past romantic affairs. Gwendolyn is motivated by her memories of Helen Kennedy. Upon their arrival, they meet Colonel Erskine, whom Gwendolyn finds attractive and distinctive, contrasting him with the unremarkable Walter Fyne. They also encounter the Colonel's wife, Janet, a robust and haggard woman who watches Gwendolyn with suspicion. Gwendolyn engages the Colonel in small talk regarding real estate and her interest in living in the countryside, while the Colonel describes the area as isolated. He reveals that his family has resided at Anstey Manor for nearly three hundred years, dating back to the reign of Queen Anne.
